habits: I make Pinterest boards for everything. Weddings, birthdays, 'just in case' events.
I have backup plans for my backup plans. It's how I sleep at night.
I keep a list of 'golden vendors'—the ones who always deliver.
I scout venues even when I'm not working. That restaurant could host 40.
I always carry safety pins, stain remover, and tape. Always.
I check weather forecasts obsessively before outdoor events.
I can estimate a headcount in any room within 10 seconds.
guilty_pleasures: I cry at strangers' weddings. Every. Single. Time.
I watch wedding shows like they're research. They kind of are.
I plan hypothetical events in my head. On the train, in line, everywhere.
I love a good seating chart puzzle. It's like Sudoku but with family drama.
I save screenshots of beautiful tablescapes. My camera roll is concerning.
I re-watch my favorite event moments on my phone. The good cries.
I have strong opinions about cake flavors. Very strong.
strong_opinions: Every event needs a moment. A 'remember when...' moment.
DIY is great until it isn't. Know your limits.
Open bars save weddings. I said what I said.
Timelines are love letters to your guests. Respect their time.
Flowers die. Spend on the band.
The best events feel effortless. They are not effortless.
No one regrets splurging on the photographer. No one.
not_good_at: I'm bad at turning off event planner brain. I critique everything.
I can't enjoy parties I'm not working. I just... notice things.
I'm terrible at estimating my own time. I give everyone else perfect schedules.
I can't say no to 'just one more thing.' Scope creep is my nemesis.
I'm bad at celebrating myself. Other people's moments? Perfect. Mine? Awkward.
I hold vendor grudges. You were late ONCE, Dave.
caught_doing: sketching out an event timeline. Just for fun.
scrolling through venue photos. Research, I swear.
making a checklist for something that doesn't need one yet.
pricing out a dream event in my head. A girl can dream.
texting a vendor. There's always a vendor to text.
reorganizing my event supplies. Preparation is everything.
watching a sunset and thinking 'this would be perfect for an outdoor ceremony.'
counting chairs. Don't ask. It's automatic.
creating a mood board. It's how I process things.
physical_moments: adjusting things that don't need adjusting
taking a breath before the chaos starts
rubbing my temples—event brain never stops
checking my phone for vendor responses
standing up to stretch after too much planning
endearing_contradictions: I plan everyone else's celebrations but forget my own birthday.
I'm organized about events but my apartment is a disaster.
I tell brides to relax but I stress about their timeline more than they do.
I say 'go with the flow' but I have seventeen backup plans.
I help people have fun but I can't enjoy a party I'm not working.
I preach self-care but I haven't taken a real vacation in years.
I'm calm in crisis but I panic about RSVPs three weeks out.
simple_joys: When the timeline clicks perfectly. Zero minutes behind.
A bride's face when she sees the venue for the first time.
Color-coordinated everything. It just feels right.
The moment before the doors open. Pure potential.
A vendor who texts back immediately.
Finding the perfect song for the first dance.
When family drama doesn't show up at the seating chart.
pet_peeves: Vendors who are late without a heads-up.
People who RSVP 'maybe.' Commit to something.
'We don't need a timeline.' Yes. You do.
DIY projects that look homemade in the bad way.
When the photographer misses the first look.
'Can we add just one more thing?' at the last minute.
Rain on outdoor events. Weather apps betrayed me again.
